title: SE-Vigilant automated sensor beacon

[[Fabritech/Legends|Fabritech's]] '''SE-Vigilant automated sensor beacon''' was a lightweight [[Sensor beacon/Legends|sensor beacon]] used for the electronic surveillance of its surrounding area. 

==Description==
The SE-Vigilant was a compact and light two-piece design, consisting of two components; the main post and the upper [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] pole. The low mass of the unit allowed multiple season beacons to be carried by live mounts such as [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntauns]], [[Dewback/Legends|dewbacks]], and [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]].<ref name="EGWT" /> The beacon measured one-point-four [[Meter/Legends|meters]] when collapsed, extending to a full height of three meters when deployed.<ref name="AE" />

The main post was secured to the ground, either by using a [[magnetic disk]], or driving [[anchoring rod]]s fifty [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]] into the surface. The post was protected by electrified [[stun strip]]s that could drive off local wildlife with variable intensity [[Stun/Legends|stun]] shocks.<ref name="EGWT" />

The upper sensor pole was mounted with twin [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor arrays]], sensor [[Analytical computer/Legends|analysis computer]], and broadcast equipment. The sensor packages were mounted on a rotation joint that completed four revolutions per [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. This allowed the sensor arrays to gather complete data coverage of the surrounding area<ref name="EGWT" /> up to a range of two hundred and fifty meters.<ref name="AE" />

The upper sensor array was tasked with generally scanning the surrounding terrain, and included a long-range, wide-angle [[visual recorder]] and a [[Motion sensor/Legends|motion sensor]], along with [[Light sensor|light]] and [[heat sensor]]s. The lower array was tasked with more detailed scans and included a multipurpose visual recorder augmented with magnification, [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]], heat emission adapters, and [[Audio sensor/Legends|audio pickups]].<ref name="EGWT" />

The sensor arrays fed their data into the sensor analysis computers which automated the functions of the linked sensors. The information was transmitted to a control station, and encryption modules could be added to the beacon to scramble the transmissions. The communications gear had a broadcast range of up to fifty [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]].<ref name="EGWT" />

The entire season beacon was power by rechargeable [[Power cell/Legends|power cells]] which were automatically charged by retractable [[Solar collector/Legends|solar colllectors]]. During maintenance and calibration operations, the power cells were fully recharged, and the cells could operate continuously for three [[Standard month/Legends|months]].<ref name="EGWT" />

The SE-Vigilant had a modular design, allowing sensor arrays to be swapped out and replaced as needed. Specialized arrays could be installed onto the beacon, including ones dedicated to scanning for geothermal energy, life-forms, communication signals, and [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]].<ref name="EGWT" /> 

==History==
The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] deployed a number of SE-Vigilant units to keep watch over the frozen wastelands surrounding [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] were setting up these sensors up just prior to Skywalker's encounter with a [[Wampa/Legends|Wampa]].<ref name="EGWT" />
